description: A shape with 2+ fields, plus two actions: one that does the shape's core work (here, prints a message), and a second that calls the first. Validated against the real transpiler across 20 variations (different shape/field/action names), all parse/emit/compile clean.

fields.desc: list of [field_name, dictum_type] pairs for the shape

primary_action.desc: the action that does the shape's core work

caller_action.desc: the second action, which just calls primary_action

template:
shape {shape_name} holds
{fields}
end shape
action {primary_action} produces nothing
    print the text "{greeting}" and newline
end action
action {caller_action} produces nothing
    call {primary_action}
end action
requires: Reference a shape's fields only from inside an action that operates on a declared variable of that shape (e.g. via its parameters or a `keep`-declared instance) -- never via bracket/dot interpolation inside a string.
common_mistakes: Do not write a string like `"Hello, my name is [Person.name]"` or `"...is <name>..."` to interpolate a field's value -- Dictum has no string-interpolation syntax at all. This is not a hypothetical: a real test run produced exactly this (first with `[Person.name]`, then with `<name>` after the first form was blocked) when asked to greet using a Person shape's fields.

Do not write `set ShapeName to someField` -- assigning directly to a shape/type name instead of a declared variable of that shape is not valid. A real test run produced exactly `set Person to age` when asked to write a `main` action for a Person shape with no other guidance.
example:
shape Person holds
    name as text
    age as whole number
end shape
action greet produces nothing
    print the text "Hello" and newline
end action
action main produces nothing
    call greet
end action
